hero.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subline: Answers to the most common insurance questions. Still have questions? Call us at (718) 555-0100.

items.question: What is an independent insurance broker?
items.answer: An independent broker represents many insurance companies (called carriers) rather than just one. This means we can shop your coverage across 30+ carriers to find the best rate and coverage for your needs — unlike a captive agent who is limited to one company's products.

items.question: How long does it take to get a quote?
items.answer: Most personal lines quotes (auto, home) are ready within 2 hours during business hours. Commercial and specialty coverage may take 1–2 business days depending on complexity.

items.question: Do you charge a fee for your services?
items.answer: No. As a broker, we are compensated by the insurance carrier when you purchase a policy. Our service to you — shopping multiple carriers and advising on coverage — is free.

items.question: What states are you licensed in?
items.answer: We are licensed in New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania. We can help clients in any of these states.

items.question: Can I bundle multiple types of insurance?
items.answer: Yes, and we recommend it. Bundling auto and home insurance, for example, often results in discounts of 10–25%. We can bundle all your personal and business coverage through the same carrier when it saves you money.

items.question: What auto insurance coverage is required in New York?
items.answer: New York requires: Bodily Injury Liability (25/50/10), Personal Injury Protection (PIP), and Uninsured Motorist coverage. Comprehensive and collision are optional but usually required by lenders.

items.question: What affects my auto insurance rate?
items.answer: Key factors include: driving record, age, vehicle make/model, annual mileage, credit score (in some states), ZIP code, and coverage limits selected.

items.question: Can I get insurance for a leased vehicle?
items.answer: Yes. Leased vehicles typically require full coverage (comprehensive and collision) plus gap insurance, which covers the difference between what you owe and what the car is worth if totaled.

items.question: What is a BOP (Business Owner's Policy)?
items.answer: A BOP bundles general liability insurance with commercial property insurance into one convenient policy, typically at a lower cost than buying each separately. It's ideal for most small businesses.

items.question: Is workers compensation required in New York?
items.answer: Yes. New York requires all employers — even those with just one employee — to carry workers compensation insurance. Penalties for non-compliance can be severe.

items.question: What is a Certificate of Insurance (COI)?
items.answer: A COI is a document that summarizes your insurance coverage and is often required by clients, landlords, or general contractors before you can start work. We issue COIs quickly — usually same day.

items.question: What is TLC insurance?
items.answer: TLC insurance is commercial auto insurance specifically required by the NYC Taxi and Limousine Commission for for-hire vehicles including taxis, Uber, Lyft, and private car services.

items.question: How quickly can I get TLC insurance?
items.answer: We specialize in TLC insurance and can often bind coverage the same day. We understand TLC deadlines and compliance requirements.

items.question: Do you have Spanish and Chinese-speaking agents for TLC drivers?
items.answer: Yes. Our team includes agents who speak English, Spanish, and Mandarin/Cantonese — many of our TLC clients prefer to work in their native language.
